index.title: IBD Patient Stories | Living With Crohn's & Colitis | IBDPal
index.description: Real-world stories from people using IBDPal for Crohn's disease and ulcerative colitis, shared with consent for education only.
index.intro: These vignettes are shared with permission, de-identified, and for encouragement only, not medical outcomes data.

stories.name: Maya
stories.headline: Tracking protein and hydration before infusion days
stories.quote: Tracking protein and hydration before infusion days helped me stop guessing what to tell my nurse. IBDPal logs made my appointments shorter and clearer.
paragraphs: Maya was diagnosed with Crohn's disease in her late twenties. Between infusion appointments she wanted a simple way to remember meals, fluids, and symptom trends without carrying a notebook.
She logs breakfast protein, water intake, and any cramping in IBDPal during the week before each infusion. At the chair, she exports a one-page summary for her nurse.
Her team still makes all treatment decisions. The logs simply help her arrive prepared and ask better questions about nutrition labs and fatigue.

stories.name: James
stories.headline: Showing my GI what the flare week looked like
stories.quote: During a flare I used the low-residue articles on the blog and my symptom log to show my GI what the week looked like. We adjusted meds faster.
paragraphs: James manages ulcerative colitis while working full time. When urgency increased, he worried his description at the visit would not match what he lived through at home.
He read IBDPal's low-residue and flare articles for general education, then tracked stools per day, blood, and foods in the app for five days.
The export gave his gastroenterologist a clearer timeline. Medication changes still followed clinic protocols; James says the log reduced back-and-forth and stress.

stories.name: Priya
stories.headline: Sleep, stress, and finals week
stories.quote: ImproveCareNow pointed us to a pediatric center; IBDPal helped our son notice sleep and stress patterns before finals week.
paragraphs: Priya's teenager was diagnosed with IBD during high school. The family connected with ImproveCareNow for pediatric resources and a 504 planning letter from their GI.
They use IBDPal together to note sleep hours, stress spikes, and school absences without turning logging into a grade. Patterns before finals helped them ask about pacing and hydration early.
Priya emphasizes that peer networks and clinicians lead care decisions. The app supports conversations at home and before nurse check-ins.
